findings.message: Skill body is a placeholder stub with no instructional content; version 0.0.2, user-invocable: false
user-invocable: false requires auto-trigger context in the description, but with an empty body the skill has no activation path — neither manual slash-command nor auto-trigger works

Body has minimal standalone instructional content; names library categories but defers all depth to reference files, leaving the SKILL.md body too thin to guide decisions without the references loaded

Description trigger 'when choosing libraries' is broad and risks loading the skill on any Go session involving library decisions, flooding context with library catalogs when a specific skill would suffice
Skill body is a curated resource/link list with no code examples, no procedural instructions, and no actionable guidance
Skill has no persona directive; combined with a resource-list body, the model has no framing context for how to interpret or apply the linked resources
Cross-References section links to samber/cc-skills-golang@golang-google-wire which has no corresponding skill directory
Two skills reference samber/cc-skills-golang@golang-google-wire which does not exist; likely a planned skill never created
Cross-References section links to samber/cc-skills-golang@golang-google-wire which has no corresponding skill directory

Skill body is a placeholder stub with no instructional content; version 0.0.2, user-invocable: false — Body lines 1-7 contain only the placeholder string '(Content will be added in a future iteration)'; no headings, code, or instructional text present

Cross-References section links to samber/cc-skills-golang@golang-google-wire which has no corresponding skill directory — skills/golang-google-wire/ directory does not exist in the repository; reference appears in Cross-References section

Cross-References section links to samber/cc-skills-golang@golang-google-wire which has no corresponding skill directory — skills/golang-google-wire/ directory does not exist in the repository; identical dangling reference in uber-fx Cross-References section confirms copy-paste from shared template
Skill body is a curated resource/link list with no code examples, no procedural instructions, and no actionable guidance

Body has minimal standalone instructional content; names library categories but defers all depth to reference files, leaving the SKILL.md body too thin to guide decisions without the references loaded

user-invocable: false requires auto-trigger context in the description, but with an empty body the skill has no activation path — neither manual slash-command nor auto-trigger works — Skill has user-invocable: false and an empty body; there is no description trigger or body content to signal when the skill should auto-load

Two skills reference samber/cc-skills-golang@golang-google-wire which does not exist; likely a planned skill never created — skills/golang-google-wire/ not present; both golang-uber-dig and golang-uber-fx reference it with identical wording, suggesting a planned but unimplemented skill
Skill has no persona directive; combined with a resource-list body, the model has no framing context for how to interpret or apply the linked resources

Description trigger 'when choosing libraries' is broad and risks loading the skill on any Go session involving library decisions, flooding context with library catalogs when a specific skill would suffice
